intro: Stelis carnosilabia, also known as the "mealy Stelis," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique, hairy leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Stelis carnosilabia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in dried powder form, to take advantage of its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
